    "Vacuum decay test failed",
    "Micron level rose rapidly from 475 to 3138 microns in 10 minutes",
    "Pressure rise above 500 microns indicates a leak or severe moisture contamination"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Perform a pressure test with dry nitrogen to locate the leak",
    "Repair any identified leaks in the brazed joints or connections",
    "Re-evacuate the system and repeat the standing vacuum decay test"
  ],
  "rationale": "The system was evacuated to 475 microns, but upon isolating the pump, the pressure rose to 3138 microns in 10 minutes. A rise above 500 microns (and specifically continuing past 1000-1500 microns to over 3000 microns) indicates a physical leak in the system rather than simple moisture outgassing.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 608",
    "Industry Standard Evacuation Practices"
  ]
